content.text: ) is a TypeScript library that provides a unified interface for text generation, streaming, structured output, and embeddings across multiple LLM providers. It abstracts OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, Groq, Bedrock, and others behind identical function signatures, so switching providers requires changing a single import.
The SDK is provider-agnostic by design. Model providers live in separate packages (
, etc.) and are passed as arguments to the core functions, not configured globally.

attrs.alt: Vercel AI SDK core primitives: generateText, streamText, generateObject on top of model provider abstraction and framework integrations

content.text:
npm install ai @ai-sdk/openai
# or for Anthropic:
npm install ai @ai-sdk/anthropic
). Provider-specific packages contain the model factories. Install only the providers you use.

sends a prompt to the model and returns the complete response once generation finishes. Use it for non-streaming use cases: server-side data extraction, batch processing, and anywhere you need the full text before doing anything with it:
if the model invoked tools. All fields are typed.
awaits the entire response before returning. For user-facing interfaces where you want text to appear progressively, use
array to send conversation history. Each message has a

field is cleaner when the system prompt is a fixed string.
. It returns immediately with a result object containing async iterables for text chunks and full events:
that yields each text chunk as it arrives. You can also
to wait for the complete text, or use
to pipe the stream to a Next.js or Express response.
forces the model to produce output that satisfies a Zod schema. The SDK validates the response and retries automatically on schema violations:
is fully typed — TypeScript infers the type from the Zod schema. No manual
Server-side extraction where you need the full result before proceeding
UI where you want to render fields as they stream in
streams the JSON as it generates, yielding progressively more-complete partial objects. This is ideal for UI components that render data incrementally:
Each partial object may have missing or undefined fields until generation is complete. Null-check before accessing nested fields — use the
Provider packages expose factory functions that create model instances. The call signature of every SDK function stays identical when you swap providers — only the model argument changes:

This is the primary advantage of using the AI SDK over calling provider APIs directly. A/B testing between models, fallback strategies, and routing logic all work without changing core application code.
hooks that manage streaming responses and chat history for React components. Pair them with an edge route that calls
manages the message list, the current input value, streaming state, and error handling.
posts the messages to the configured API route, receives the stream, and appends each chunk to the last assistant message in real time.
Language model middleware wraps a model instance to add cross-cutting behaviour — logging, caching, reasoning extraction, or prompt modification.
applies middleware without changing any call site:
looks for content wrapped in the specified XML tag and exposes it on
separately from the final answer. Other built-in middleware includes
for providers that don't support streaming natively.
for vector embedding workflows. Use the same provider abstraction pattern as for text generation:
Embed a single string — returns a Float32Array
Embed multiple strings in one request — more efficient than looping
Measure semantic similarity between two embedding vectors (0–1)
Comparing AI SDK to Direct Provider APIs

inside a streaming route handler blocks the response until generation completes. Use
to stream directly into the HTTP response.
sends the Zod schema to the model as a JSON schema description. Very large or deeply nested schemas can confuse the model. Start with flat schemas and add nesting only when needed.
Provider packages must be installed separately from
throws a runtime error, not a TypeScript error.
Token usage is not available in real-time during streaming; it is only populated after the stream ends (
). Do not try to read it mid-stream.
to give the model the ability to call functions. Each tool is defined with a
is provided, the SDK calls it automatically and returns the results alongside the generated text:
from a tool definition, the SDK returns the raw
array without executing them — useful when you need to dispatch tool calls to a separate service or add approval logic before execution.
to enable multi-step agentic loops where the model can call tools multiple times in a single
invocation, automatically feeding tool results back into the next model call.
to cancel an in-flight request. This is essential for streaming UIs where the user may navigate away, or for server-side timeout enforcement:
The signal is forwarded to the underlying HTTP client. Cancelling a streaming response stops token delivery immediately; the partial text up to cancellation is not returned.
The AI SDK retries on rate limit (
) responses with exponential backoff. Control this with the
retries twice before throwing. Set it to
to disable retries entirely (useful in test environments where you want failures to propagate immediately).
returns the complete text at once;
yields tokens progressively — choose based on whether the caller needs the full response before acting.
combines generation and schema validation: the SDK retries automatically on schema violations.
, etc.) are separate npm packages; swapping providers is a one-line change.
handle streaming, history, and error state for React UIs.
adds cross-cutting behaviour (logging, caching, reasoning extraction) without changing call sites.
provide first-class embedding and similarity search support.
